summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service by asking the congregation a central question: 'Is God first?' He emphasized that every spiritually mature Christian has discovered that unless God is first, we will not receive everything He has for our lives. The message explored the difference between treating God as a good-luck charm or a genie versus having a genuine, priority relationship with Him. Pastor Travis used the analogy of a pie, explaining that Jesus doesn't want to be just one slice of our lives but the filling throughout the entire pie, present in every area. He grounded the teaching in the first commandment from Exodus 20:3, highlighting God's desire to be our sole priority. The sermon provided a practical framework through four key questions to evaluate if God is truly first: in our schedule, finances, abilities, and relationships. Pastor Travis explained the biblical principle of tithing as the 'first 10%' and a test of trust, referencing Malachi 3:10. He also stressed the importance of the Sabbath as a day of rest and worship, and the need to choose close friends who sharpen us spiritually and share our convictions. The call was to move from merely looking like a Christian to being the 'real deal' by making God the undisputed center of our lives, promising that this alignment unlocks greater joy, peace, and divine blessing.
mainPoints: God must be first in our schedule and time, which includes a daily quiet time and honoring the Sabbath as a day of rest and worship.
God must be first in our finances, demonstrated through the biblical practice of tithing the first 10% as a test of trust and priority.
God must be first in our relationships, meaning we use our gifts to serve others and choose close friends who draw us closer to Christ.

versesMentioned.text: You shall have no other gods before me.
for I, the LORD your God, am a jealous God...
Teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.
But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
Remember the Sabbath day by keeping it holy. Six days you shall labor and do all your work, but the seventh day is a sabbath to the LORD your God.

Eat the tithe of your grain, new wine and olive oil, and the firstborn of your herds and flocks in the presence of the LORD your God at the place he will choose as a dwelling for his Name, so that you may learn to revere the LORD your God always.
A tithe of everything from the land, whether grain from the soil or fruit from the trees, belongs to the LORD; it is holy to the LORD.
Bring the best of the firstfruits of your soil to the house of the LORD your God.

Will a mere mortal rob God? Yet you rob me. But you ask, 'How are we robbing you?' In tithes and offerings. You are under a curse—your whole nation—because you are robbing me. Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. Test me in this,' says the LORD Almighty, 'and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing that there will not be room enough to store it.
Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord, not for human masters.
Each of you should use whatever gift you have received to serve others, as faithful stewards of God’s grace in its various forms.
As iron sharpens iron, so one person sharpens another.
As water reflects the face, so one’s life reflects the heart.
Do not be yoked together with unbelievers. For what do righteousness and wickedness have in common? Or what fellowship can light have with darkness?
One who has unreliable friends soon comes to ruin, but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.

Monday.scripture: Matthew 6:33 - 'But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.'
Monday.action: Before checking your phone or starting work, spend 10 minutes in prayer and reading the Bible.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to seek You first today in my thoughts, time, and decisions.

Tuesday.scripture: Exodus 20:8 - 'Remember the Sabbath day by keeping it holy.'
Tuesday.action: Intentionally block out time this coming Sunday for worship and rest. Plan to disconnect from work.
Tuesday.prayer: God, teach me the spiritual discipline of rest. Help me to honor Your Sabbath.

Wednesday.scripture: Malachi 3:10 - 'Test me in this,' says the LORD Almighty, 'and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven...'
Wednesday.action: Review your budget or bank statement. Prayerfully commit to giving God the first 10% of your next income.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I trust You as my provider. I choose to put You first with my finances.

Thursday.scripture: Colossians 3:23 - 'Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord...'
Thursday.action: Identify one task today and do it with excellence, as if Jesus Himself were your supervisor.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, let my work today be an act of worship, using the gifts You've given me for Your glory.

Friday.scripture: Proverbs 27:17 - 'As iron sharpens iron, so one person sharpens another.'
Friday.action: Reach out to a friend who encourages your faith. Send them an encouraging text or pray for them.
Friday.prayer: God, thank You for friends who sharpen me. Help me to be that kind of friend to others.

Saturday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 6:14 - 'Do not be yoked together with unbelievers...'
Saturday.action: Reflect on your closest relationships. Ask if they draw you toward or away from God. Commit any concerns to prayer.
Saturday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me wisdom in my friendships. Help me to be equally yoked in my closest relationships.
